_comment: 2026 Iran war — US/Israel joint strike on Iran (Feb 28 weekend), OVX pre-strike close 64.68 on Feb 27. Window absorption axis: OVX was already elevated (50s) due to pre-war tension, so z-score only 2.09 despite absolute deviation of +10.61 above the 20-day median. SRS CLS-001 ECDF severity = 17/20 = 0.85 (rank of |deviation| among 20 window deviations). Current tanh implementation produces ~0.10 — test will FAIL until ECDF is implemented. This is intentional: the band follows the SRS, not the code.

source.description: CBOE Crude Oil ETF Volatility Index (daily close)

expected_band.rationale_pending_trader: SRS CLS-001 ECDF: median(window)=54.075, |deviation|=|64.68-54.075|=10.605. Deviations of all 20 window values from median: 17 of 20 are ≤ 10.605, so ecdf_rank=17, severity=17/20=0.85. This is exact integer arithmetic — no tolerance needed, band is [0.85, 0.85]. Key teaching axis: despite the absolute move being large, the window was already elevated (pre-war tension kept OVX in the 50s for all of Jan-Feb 2026), meaning 3 historical deviations actually exceed the event deviation. The ECDF correctly captures this as high-but-not-extreme at 0.85. The current tanh implementation (~0.10) understates the event by ~8×. Contrast with market_data_ovx_aramco_attack_2019_09_16 (clean baseline). PENDING: /trader signoff on 0.85 band and geopolitical context.
